headline_of_the_day: India's first private rocket reaches orbit on debut launch

key_points.text: India's first privately developed rocket reached orbit on its first attempt, marking a historic milestone for the country's commercial space industry.
A lawsuit against xAI tests whether companies can be held liable for harmful content generated by their AI chatbots.
Boeing announced plans to be ready to fund development of a new airplane program by 2030, though no concrete timeline or commitments were provided.
A Georgia sheriff's deputy was charged with misusing automated license plate cameras, and several other officers in the state were fired for similar violations.

stories.title: Readers ask for favorite blogs that are not about artificial intelligence
stories.summary: A discussion thread highlights nostalgia for the era when personal blogs covered niche projects and topics beyond AI.

stories.title: Jurassic Park used four million dollars of real computer hardware in 1993
stories.summary: The film featured legitimate high-end computing equipment from the early 1990s rather than props.

stories.title: Boeing says it will fund new plane program by 2030
stories.summary: The aerospace manufacturer announced it expects to have financing ready for a new aircraft development effort within six years.

stories.title: Tech companies exploring data centers on Native American land
stories.summary: Major technology firms are reportedly pursuing plans to build data center facilities on tribal territories, though details remain unclear.

stories.title: India launches first privately developed rocket into orbit
stories.summary: The successful debut marks the first time a private Indian company has placed a rocket in orbit, expanding the country's commercial space capabilities.

stories.title: Mathematician finds counterexample to decades-old Jacobian Conjecture
stories.summary: A researcher using the Claude AI system produced a counterexample to an unsolved problem in algebraic geometry.

stories.title: xAI faces first lawsuit over chatbot-generated content
stories.summary: A case against the company asks courts to determine whether AI developers are legally responsible for harmful output their systems produce.

stories.title: Georgia deputy charged with license plate camera misuse
stories.summary: A DeKalb County officer faces charges for improperly accessing automated surveillance systems, and additional officers were terminated for similar conduct.

briefly.title: US health agency says cyclospora finding at produce company was false positive
AI should have senior lawyers sharpening their hunting spears
Trump to fund MAGA-aligned projects in Europe as he reorders US aid
